page: Amazon Web Services inf2.8xlarge is a virtualized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. The instance provides 32 dedicated vCPUs derived from 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or discrete GPUs are bundled with this instance type.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Passmark CPU encryption tests scoring in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench scores, and Redis SET operations fall within the average tier. Memory performance is robust, highlighted by a strong Passmark memory score, though uncached memory bandwidth read and write speeds remain in the average category. For language model workloads, prompt processing and text generation using a small 135M model perform at average speeds, whereas processing a large 70B model drops to the bottom 25% tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high-speed DDR4 memory provide a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and discrete GPU hardware represents a significant tradeoff for storage-heavy or hardware-accelerated machine learning tasks. This instance is best suited for workloads such as general-purpose web serving, database operations, software compilation, and moderate-scale CPU-based inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services inf2.8xlarge is a Nitro-based virtual server featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz, this x86_64 instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance and encryption capabilities, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics. It lacks local storage and discrete GPUs, presenting a tradeoff for data-intensive or hardware-accelerated tasks. Qualitatively, the instance offers a balanced resource profile suitable for general-purpose applications. It is well-suited for web serving, database operations, software compilation, and moderate CPU-based language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services inf2.8xlarge is a Nitro-based instance with 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB DDR4 RAM, delivering strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services inf2.8xlarge features 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, offering balanced general-purpose performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC virtual server with balanced memory and strong single-core performance for general-purpose computing and moderate inference workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor at 3.6 GHz
128.0 GB of DDR4 memory with 17 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong single-core CPU and encryption benchmark performance
No bundled local storage or discrete GPU hardware
Best suited for general-purpose web serving, databases, and moderate CPU inference
